# ipmi_channel_access (Resource)

Manage Set Channel Access for one channel — controls whether IPMI-over-LAN is enabled, what authentication is required, and the max privilege level.

**Lockout warning:** setting `access_mode = "disabled"` on channel 1 (the standard LAN channel) will lock Terraform out of the BMC. This resource requires an explicit `force_lockout_risk = true` to allow it.



<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `access_mode` (String) When this channel accepts sessions: `disabled`, `pre_boot`, `always`, `shared`.
- `channel` (Number) IPMI channel number (1-11). The standard LAN channel is 1.

### Optional

- `cipher_suite` (Number)
- `force_lockout_risk` (Boolean) Set to true to override the channel-1 self-lockout guard. Without it, disabling LAN access on the channel Terraform uses is blocked.
- `host` (String)
- `interface` (String)
- `password` (String, Sensitive)
- `pef_alerting` (Boolean) If true, Platform Event Filter alerting is enabled on this channel.
- `per_message_auth` (Boolean) If true, every IPMI message is authenticated. Default true (safer).
- `persistence` (String) Where to write: `volatile` (active only), `non_volatile` (boot defaults), or `both` (default). Reads always return the volatile/active settings.
- `port` (Number)
- `privilege_limit` (String) Max privilege level granted on this channel: `callback`, `user`, `operator`, `administrator`, `oem`.
- `user_level_auth` (Boolean) If true, user-level commands require authentication. Default true (safer).
- `username` (String)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `last_updated` (String)
